transcription.status: VERIFIED (Session 13, D13.0): numbers exact via blind dual entry; judgments ratified by Hassan
transcription.verification_method: blind dual machine transcription + human ratification of judgments
transcription.verification_note: Blind dual entry (Session 13, D13.0): the PDF was rendered to page images and Tables 2-3 transcribed TWICE independently by two agents that never saw this JSON, then diffed against each other and against this file. RESULT — numeric data is exact: all 26 Policy-Space rows (Table 2, pp.125-126) and all 34 Competitive-Space rows (Table 3, pp.130-131) match on position, capability, and salience; the two blind reads agree on every cell; the printed Exercised Power = Influence x Salience / 100 column reproduces on every row (used as a checksum); the JSON's capability = the paper's Influence column; the derived Exercised Power column is correctly omitted. Continua are faithful to the paper: case 1 to Figure 1 (p.129); case 2 to Figure 6 (p.138), whose printed markers are 0=status quo, 10=end of patronage, 30=transparent tendering, 60='Divest major identified assets; e.g. pipelines' (open access), 100=break up CNPC — i.e. the Position-60 marker the long-run coding leans on IS printed in the paper. Both published_model_forecast notes match the paper's results (Policy converges ~30, sweep 15-45; Competitive Position 20-40, sweep 30-50). OPEN JUDGMENTS FOR HASSAN (not decided by the machine): (a) the near-term outcome codings 25/25; (b) the long-run codings 55/60, incl. whether the Dec-2019 PipeChina divestiture = the paper's printed Position-60 marker; (c) the horizon rule — NOTE the paper states NO calendar horizon (its 10 'turns' are bargaining rounds, not years; the only temporal phrase is 'in the near term'), so the by_2017 / by_2020 / 5-year framing below is an imposed convention, not the paper's own. RATIFIED by Hassan (Session 13, 2026-07-21): (a) near-term codings 25/25 = 'Ratify both = 25'; (b) long-run codings 55/60 = 'Ratify 55 / 60' — Dec-2019 PipeChina divestiture confirmed as the paper's printed Position-60 marker; (c) horizon = 'Keep as our convention' — the by_2017/by_2020 readings are kept but relabeled as OUR scoring convention, since the paper states no calendar horizon. verified flipped to true on this ratification.

cases.title: Private-sector space in China's upstream oil & gas (policy dimension)
cases.source: Efird, Lester & Wise (2016), 'Analyzing Coalitions in China's Policy Formulation', Journal of East Asian Studies 16, 117-145, doi:10.1017/jea.2015.4, Table 2

continuum.label: Openness of upstream oil & gas to private participation
continuum.anchor_100: Policy environment open and conducive to private participation

markers.15: private allowed only in JV with an SOE (approx. status quo 2014)
markers.30: ad hoc participation by government approval

published_model_forecast.value_note: convergence around the Xi-led cluster at ~30; little prospect of opening (paper text)

near_term_by_2017.basis: RATIFIED by Hassan (Session 13): little near-term opening; participation remained JV/approval-based; consistent with the paper's near-term claim. Horizon (~2017) is our scoring convention, not the paper's stated horizon.

long_run_by_2020.basis: RATIFIED by Hassan (Session 13): May 2020 Ministry of Natural Resources opened oil & gas exploration/production licensing to qualified domestic private and foreign firms — a structural opening, though practical penetration stayed limited. Horizon (~2020) is our 5-year scoring convention.

continuum.label: Degree of reform reducing CNPC's dominance

markers.60: required divestiture of major assets, e.g. pipelines run on open access

published_model_forecast.value_note: reform reflecting a Position between 20 and 40; breakup implausible; scenario sweep bounds outcome ~30-50 even under extreme leader positions (paper text)

near_term_by_2017.basis: RATIFIED by Hassan (Session 13): anti-corruption purges of CNPC leadership, tendering/mixed-ownership pilots, no structural divestiture. Horizon (~2017) is our scoring convention.

long_run_by_2020.basis: RATIFIED by Hassan (Session 13): Dec 2019 creation of PipeChina; CNPC (with Sinopec/CNOOC) pipeline assets divested into a national open-access pipeline company, operational Oct 2020 — confirmed as matching the paper's explicit Figure-6 Position-60 marker ('Divest major identified assets; e.g. pipelines' on open access); CNPC core not broken up. Horizon (~2020) is our 5-year scoring convention.

notes: DOMAIN CAVEAT: these are domestic-elite bargaining cases (cooperative regime), NOT coercive interstate crises — they extend out-of-domain validation beyond the EU but do NOT settle the coercive verdict. The coercive classics (Red Flag over Hong Kong, Iran 1984, Feder cases) remain the quest.

HORIZON RULE (D13.0, ratified as OUR scoring convention): the paper states no calendar horizon — its 10 'turns' are bargaining rounds, not years, and it only says 'in the near term'. So each case carries two outcome readings under our own convention: a near-term reading (~2017, primary, used for the gate) and a 5-year reading (~2020, secondary, reported not gated). These horizons are ours, not the paper's.
BONUS COLUMN: the published KTAB forecast enables three-way scoring on these cases: Schelling challenge vs compromise mean vs the incumbent KTAB SMP.
